head.hi: The Ambassador in France (Dillon) to the Department of State

div.p: 1634. Repeated information USUN 10 London 453. Reference: Deptel 1418, October 16.Supra.

Department’s concern as indicated reference telegram conveyed frankly to ParodiAlexandre Parodi, Secretary General of the French Ministry of Foreign Affairs. today. He replied immediately Moch was acting on his own as usual and was difficult to control. He said MendesPierre Mendès-France, Premier of France. had yesterday been irritated with Moch over an article in London Observer (which we have not been able to identify) and had sent him a sharp telegram last night. In response to our inquiry as to what might be done to control Moch, Parodi said he did not know but would consult Mendes. He also suggested that I take it up directly with latter which I will do at first opportunity.
